title: Bar Chart: How frequently people in the USA ate in fast food restaurants between 2003 and 2013
description: The chart below shows how frequently people in the USA ate in fast food restaurants between 2003 and 2013. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features and making comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Fast food frequency in the USA (2003–2013)

TID sẽ chỉ bạn cách xử lý dạng frequency chart (tần suất) theo nhiều năm – dạng này rất hay ra nha.

1. Hiểu đề bài

Biểu đồ cho thấy tần suất ăn fast food của người Mỹ qua 3 năm:

  • 2003
  • 2006
  • 2013

các mức độ gồm:

  • Every day
  • Several times a week
  • Once a week
  • Once or twice a month
  • A few times a year
  • Never

TID nhắc bạn:

Dạng này không chỉ là tăng/giảm, mà là phân bố thói quen.

2. Cách viết Introduction

Paraphrase đề, giữ:

  • frequency of eating fast food
  • USA
  • 3 năm

3. Cách viết Overview

TID gợi ý overview:

  • Phần lớn người ăn fast food ở mức trung bình (once a week / once or twice a month)
  • Tỷ lệ ăn hàng ngày và không bao giờ ăn luôn rất thấp
  • Xu hướng chuyển nhẹ từ ăn hàng tuần sang ăn theo tháng vào năm 2013

4. Cách chia Body

BODY 1 – Nhóm phổ biến (main habits)
  • Once a week:

31 → 33 → 27 (cao nhất, nhưng giảm về 2013)

  • Once or twice a month:

30 → 25 → 33 (tăng và trở thành cao nhất năm 2013)

TID chốt:

→ đây là 2 nhóm dominant

BODY 2 – Nhóm còn lại
  • Several times a week:

16 → 19 → 15 (tăng rồi giảm)

  • A few times a year:

12 → 14 → 14 (tăng nhẹ rồi ổn định)

  • Every day:

3 → 2 → 2 (rất thấp)

  • Never:

4 → 3 → 3 (cũng rất thấp)

TID chốt:

→ hai nhóm extreme (every day / never) luôn thấp

5. Gợi ý paraphrase

  • frequency
  • the majority of people
  • peaked at
  • declined slightly
  • remained low

Ví dụ:

The majority of people consumed fast food either once a week or once or twice a month nha.

The proportion eating fast food every day remained very low throughout the period.

The bar chart compares how frequently people in the USA ate at fast food restaurants in 2003, 2006 and 2013.

Overall, the most common eating habits were once a week and once or twice a month, while eating every day or never was consistently rare. Over time, there was a slight shift away from weekly visits towards monthly visits.

In 2003, the largest proportion of people ate fast food once a week (31%), followed closely by once or twice a month (30%). By 2006, weekly eating became even more dominant, rising to 33%, while monthly visits dropped to 25%. However, in 2013, this pattern reversed: once or twice a month became the most popular frequency at 33%, whereas once a week declined to 27%.

Less frequent habits showed smaller changes. The percentage of people eating fast food several times a week peaked at 19% in 2006 before falling to 15% in 2013. Those eating a few times a year increased gradually from 12% to 14%. Meanwhile, daily consumption remained very low (around 2–3%), and the proportion who never ate fast food stayed minimal at about 3–4% throughout the period.
